name: John LaPorta
abstract: John Daniel LaPorta (April 13, 1920 – May 12, 2004) was a jazz clarinetist and composer.

interviews.title: John LaPorta interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 13, 1996

works.t: Strike Up the Band: The Man I Love

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Mel Torme with Herb Pomeroy Big Band and Gerry Mulligan/ Gerry Mulligan Sextet

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Bergonzi
about_quotes.evidence: And I was in John LaPorta’s Youth Band at the Greater Boston area.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Bergonzi interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 7, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: Marshall would get those guys in to tutor the band... we’d have LaPorta would write original stuff for the band... John LaPorta and Lou Mucci would come in and rehearse, tutor the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I was doing a lot of those things, like stage-band clinics with a really nice group. ... John LaPorta. ... That would be the faculty.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder Part 1
about_quotes.evidence: I went to the Mastbaum School in Philadelphia with Buddy DeFranco and Red Rodney, and a bunch of guys. And John LaPorta.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder Part 1 interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Rusty Dedrick
about_quotes.evidence: That’s John LaPorta. ... John is a very complex man. Excellent musician.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rusty Dedrick interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: Then it was the Berklee people who came: John LaPorta and those guys
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
